mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and federal funds rate hikes creates stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate profit margins and compresses equity multiples, driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both rise, with positive returns occurring in >55% of cases over a 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening real economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which supports equity valuations and generates positive forward returns during recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, with negative returns dominating >50% of cases or Rank IC falling below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income growth fuels consumer spending expectations, boosting near-term corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods where RSAFS increases, or the signal shows no statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts signals collapsing construction demand and tightening credit conditions, which precedes broader economic slowdowns and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts falling below the 5% threshold does not precede negative 20-day returns, with positive returns occurring in >65% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment indicates robust labor demand without wage-price spirals, supporting consumer confidence and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined payroll growth and stable unemployment signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive significance with Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high inflation erodes real household purchasing power and forces aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative forward returns during tightening regimes.
failure_condition: High CPIAUCSL levels fail to predict negative 60-day returns during tightening cycles, with positive returns dominating >60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03.
